app.name: Aram
app.tagline: Resources for stronger communities. Built with you, not for you.
app.disclaimer: This is not a medical or psychological assessment. If you are in crisis, please contact a health professional.
app.howToUseBody: Aram is a community resource platform with seven pillars of support. Tap any pillar below to explore resources, guides, and tools. Use the language toggle (EN/த) at the top to switch between English and Tamil. All content works offline once loaded.

assistant.title: Ask Aram
assistant.subtitle: Your bilingual guide to resources and support
assistant.placeholder: Ask anything in English or Tamil...
assistant.disclaimer: This assistant helps you find resources and answers. It is not a substitute for medical, legal, or professional advice. If you are in crisis, call Sumithrayo on +94 11 268 2535 (24/7) or dial 1990 for emergencies.
assistant.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
assistant.notConfigured: The assistant is not configured yet.

wellbeing.title: Wellbeing
wellbeing.subtitle: Holistic mental, emotional, and spiritual wellbeing support
wellbeing.understandingBody: Wellbeing is not just the absence of illness. It is feeling safe, connected, and hopeful. After years of conflict, displacement, and loss, it is natural to carry grief and stress. Healing does not mean forgetting — it means finding ways to live with what has happened while building a life of meaning and dignity.
wellbeing.peerSupportDesc: A step-by-step guide for supporting someone in your community who is in distress
wellbeing.resourcesDesc: Organisations and services that can help
wellbeing.spiritualBody: Temple, prayer, community gathering, and cultural practice have always been sources of strength for Tamil communities. Healing is not only clinical — it is also found in ritual, in song, in the support of elders, and in the rhythms of daily life.
wellbeing.selfAssessmentDesc: A simple, private check-in to reflect on how you are feeling. This is not a diagnostic tool.
wellbeing.q1: I feel safe in my day-to-day life
wellbeing.q2: I have people I can talk to when things are difficult
wellbeing.q3: I am able to sleep and rest
wellbeing.q4: I feel hopeful about the future
wellbeing.q5: I am able to do things that matter to me
wellbeing.resultLow: It sounds like things have been difficult lately. You are not alone, and it is okay to ask for help. Consider reaching out to a trusted person in your community, or one of the services listed below.
wellbeing.resultMid: You are managing, but there may be areas where more support could help. Small steps — talking to someone, taking time for rest, or reconnecting with something meaningful — can make a difference.
wellbeing.resultHigh: You seem to be in a good place. Continue doing what supports your wellbeing, and remember that it is okay to seek help if things change.

peerGuide.title: Peer Support Guide
peerGuide.subtitle: When you support someone in your community, you are doing important work. This guide will help you listen well and know when to refer someone to professional help.
peerGuide.step1Body: Give the person your full attention. Put away your phone. Make eye contact. Nod to show you are listening. Do not interrupt.
peerGuide.step2Body: "I hear you." "That sounds really difficult." "You are not alone." "Thank you for trusting me." Simple words of acknowledgement go a long way.
peerGuide.step3Body: Avoid: "Just be positive." "Others have it worse." "You should be over it by now." "Everything happens for a reason." These phrases dismiss pain and can cause harm.
peerGuide.step4Body: If someone talks about harming themselves or others, or if they seem unable to care for themselves, help them connect with a health professional or call a crisis line. You do not need to handle this alone.
peerGuide.step5Body: Supporting others is emotionally demanding. Talk to someone you trust about your own feelings. Take breaks. You cannot pour from an empty cup.

send.title: SEND
send.subtitle: Support for children and adults with Special Educational Needs and Disabilities
send.understandingBody: SEND stands for Special Educational Needs and Disabilities. Every person learns and develops differently. Some children and adults need additional support — this does not mean anything is wrong with them. Understanding SEND helps families and communities provide the right support.
send.affirming: Your child is not broken. They simply learn and experience the world differently. With understanding and the right support, every person can thrive.
send.supportAtHomeDesc: Practical tips for families supporting a child or adult with SEND
send.schoolSupportDesc: How to advocate for your child in school
send.servicesDesc: Organisations providing disability support in Sri Lanka

health.title: Healthcare
health.subtitle: Health education, awareness, and peer-to-peer signposting
health.findingCareBody: Sri Lanka has a public healthcare system accessible to all citizens. The Medical Officer of Health (MOH) system provides primary care at the divisional level. Divisional hospitals handle common conditions. Teaching hospitals in Jaffna and Batticaloa serve as referral centres for the North and East.
health.peerHealthDesc: How to help a neighbour or family member navigate the health system
health.womensHealthDesc: Maternal health, menstrual health, and menopause — with dignity and respect

community.title: Community Development
community.subtitle: Build economic resilience and entrepreneurial capability
community.economyBody: The Tamil areas of Sri Lanka have a vibrant informal economy driven by resilience and resourcefulness. Women-led microenterprises, agricultural cooperatives, and fishing communities form the backbone of local economies. Digital tools and the creator economy offer new ways to connect local production to wider markets.
community.microEnterpriseDesc: A step-by-step guide to starting a small business
community.businessModelsDesc: Community-appropriate business models for the digital age
community.financialBasicsDesc: Understanding money, bookkeeping, and accessing credit
community.storiesDesc: Stories from community members who have built something meaningful

education.adultLearningDesc: Pathways for adults who missed formal education

diaspora.title: Diaspora Reconnection
diaspora.subtitle: Support for Tamil diaspora members connecting with Sri Lanka
diaspora.contextBody: The Northern and Eastern Provinces of Sri Lanka have changed significantly since the end of the conflict. Infrastructure has been rebuilt in some areas but remains lacking in others. The economy is recovering but many families still struggle. Political dynamics remain complex. Daily life carries both progress and ongoing challenges.
diaspora.visitingDesc: Practical guidance for diaspora members returning to visit
diaspora.sendingResourcesDesc: How to send money and support safely and effectively
diaspora.connectionsDesc: Finding and connecting with organisations in Sri Lanka
diaspora.returningDesc: Guidance for diaspora considering return or investment
